title: Auto-update doesn't work using Quadlet
body:
### Issue Description

I can't use the auto-update feature for this container:
```
[Unit]
Description=Wireguard-ui container

[Container]
ContainerName=wireguard-ui
Image=docker.io/ngoduykhanh/wireguard-ui:latest
AutoUpdate=registry
AddCapability=NET_ADMIN
Environment=BIND_ADDRESS=localhost:80
PodmanArgs=--pod wireguard-pod

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

```
	* auto-updating container "f52be832eb2da504deb7f6ed482adb0247cf4f0a1d8a8db69ccee22112201d69": no PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT label found
```

But everything is OK with this:
```
[Unit]
Description=SSH-cloudflared container

[Container]
ContainerName=ssh-cloudflared
Image=docker.io/cloudflare/cloudflared:latest
AutoUpdate=registry
Secret=ssh-tunnel,type=env,target=TUNNEL_TOKEN
Network=ssh-cloudflared
Exec=tunnel run

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

```
            ssh-cloudflared.service  7b357ee8e487 (ssh-cloudflared)  docker.io/cloudflare/cloudflared:latest  registry    false
```

I also have the same problem with other containers but I think it's not necessary to show more.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create the .container file.
2. Start the container.
3. Enter `podman auto-update`. 


### Describe the results you received

 I received non-working auto-update.

### Describe the results you expected

I would like to have the working auto-update feature.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Maybe the problem is the using of pods? I don't know if it's necessary to create them another way.
```
sudo podman pod create --name wireguard-pod --network host -v wireguard-db:/app/db -v /etc/wireguard:/etc/wireguard:z
```

comments.body:
Yes, as you've stated, this is related to the container being part of a Pod. According to this: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/autoupdate/autoupdate.go#L453 once the container is part of a Pod, podman looks for the label at the Pod level and not at the container level.

So, this is a Podman general behavior and not a Quadlet one. @rhatdan @vrothberg any thoughts?
I think it is reasonable for users to expect the attribute to work if set on a container within a Pod, but want @vrothberg to chime in.

> I think it is reasonable for users to expect the attribute to work if set on a container within a Pod, but want @vrothberg to chime in.

Pods in Quadlet were meant to be used via `.kube` files.  The `PodmanArgs=--pod=xxx` works but in my eyes that really a hack.  It also implies that auto-updates with a Quadlet `.container` using this hack/workaround is not tested.

I personally prefer to not support this scenario at all and improve documentation of Quadlet/Podman.

This specific issue seems fairly easy to fix by changing the semantics of https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/autoupdate/autoupdate.go#L453 to return the container's unit (again) if the infra container is not part of another unit.  But again, there is a reason why Quadlet does not support a `Pod` field and supporting auto-updates in this scenario would mean that `.container`+pod is somehow supported.  So, although fixing the issue is probably easy, it may be better to improve the docs.

> Pods in Quadlet were meant to be used via `.kube` files

Is it rational to use K8s for my little tasks? I've never used it, so I don't know.
If you run your pod/container via the podman command and then do a `podman kube generate`, theoretically you should have a kube.yaml file ready to run within a quadlet.
